latest.title: Issue of Equity
latest.content:
Today 15:45
RNS Number : 9024Y
Atlas Metals Group PLC
31 March 2026
31 March 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of equity
Atlas Metals (LON: AMG), the natural resources and energy company announces that application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc (the "
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 648,497 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of the London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
Admission relates to the allotment of 648,497 new Ordinary Shares ("
New Shares
") to be issued to a third party in full and final settlement of a contractual claim against the Company, calculated by reference to the average prevailing 5-day volume-weighted average price of Ordinary Shares for the period ending 23 March 2026, being 7.71 pence.
Admission of the New Shares is expected to occur on 1 April 2026. The New Shares will rank
pari passu
in all respects with the existing Ordinary Shares.
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 39,181,427 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 39,181,427. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

latest.title: Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 14:57
RNS Number : 1921X
Atlas Metals Group PLC
18 March 2026
18 March 2026
Atlas Metals Group plc
("
Atlas Metals
" or the "
Company
")
Issue of New Ordinary Shares & Total Voting Rights
Atlas Metals (LSE: AMG), the natural resources and energy company announces that, further to the Company's establishment of an
"at-the-market" facility with Axis Capital Markets Limited ("
Axis
";
"
At-the-Market Facility
"
)
, application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
London Stock Exchange
") for the admission of 5,500,000 ordinary shares of nominal value £0.01 each ("
Ordinary Shares
") to be admitted to trading on the main market for listed securities of London Stock Exchange ("
Admission
").
5,500,000 Ordinary Shares will be issued to Axis at par value, in connection with the At-the-Market Facility ("
ATM Shares
"). The ATM Shares are equal to approximately 14.27% of the Company's issued Ordinary Share capital following Admission. When issued, the ATM Shares will be fully paid and will rank
pari passu
in all respects with each other and with the existing Ordinary Shares of the Company. As announced by the Company on 5 March 2026, further to the Company's entry into a funding facility with YA II PN Ltd, an institutional investor managed by Yorkville Advisors Global, LP, all proceeds from the
At-the-Market Facility will go towards repayment of this funding facility.
All Ordinary Shares previously issued pursuant to the At-the-Market Facility have been placed and Axis currently holds no Ordinary Shares.
Admission of the ATM Shares is expected to occur on or around 19 March 2026.
Total voting rights
Following Admission, the Company's issued ordinary share capital will comprise 38,532,930 Ordinary Shares, each carrying one vote. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury. The total number of voting rights in the Company following Admission will therefore be 38,532,930. With effect from Admission, this figure may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they determine whether they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Company under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.

company_30s.what_they_do: AMG.L is a small uranium exploration and development company. It focuses on identifying, acquiring, and developing uranium deposits, primarily in Kyrgyzstan, with the aim of producing feedstock for nuclear power plants.
company_30s.why_they_matter: Uranium is a critical commodity for nuclear energy, a growing segment as countries look to diversify energy mixes and reduce carbon emissions. AMG’s projects sit in a country that hosts some of the world’s largest untapped uranium reserves, giving it a potential niche advantage if it can bring production online.
company_30s.current_state: The firm is still in the exploration and early development phase, relying on modest funding rounds and a small, thin float. It is operating with a limited cash base and has not yet produced commercial uranium. The stock trades at a very low price, reflecting the high risk and speculative nature of its operations.

rns_narrative: The company is actively raising capital – a £2.5 million funding facility was announced, along with a new share issue that increased voting rights. These moves suggest a push to shore up cash for drilling and regulatory work.

There is a clear focus on the Kyrgyz uranium asset, with updates on asset status and a proposed acquisition that could expand its resource base. The company is also monitoring a potential partnership or joint venture to accelerate development.
Overall, the narrative is one of survival and incremental progress: raising money, securing rights, and positioning for a future production milestone.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet uncertainty – the community is largely silent, with only a handful of posts that hint at skepticism about the company’s ability to turn the asset into cash flow.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: None. No bullish chatter exists because the stock is perceived as too risky and illiquid for mainstream investors.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: The few voices that exist are concerned about the thin float, the lack of production history, and the high regulatory risk in Kyrgyzstan. They fear that the asset may be overvalued and that the company could fail to secure enough funding to reach production.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The mood has stayed flat; there has been no recent surge in positive or negative posts. The silence indicates that most market participants are not watching the stock closely.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is dead – no recent search interest, giving the stock maximum surprise potential if a catalyst emerges.

near_term_catalysts: Completion of the £2.5 million funding round – a cash injection would allow drilling and regulatory approvals to move forward.
Announcement of a partnership or joint‑venture that brings in technical expertise or additional capital.
First production‑ready milestone from the Kyrgyz asset, such as a successful pilot test or a feasibility study that confirms recoverable uranium.
A rise in uranium prices that makes the project more economically attractive, encouraging the company to accelerate development.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a sharp uptick in trading volume coupled with a bounce to the 7.99p support level. A sudden disclosure of a new investment partner or a regulatory approval would also be a clear green flag.
expect_next.timing_regime: The APEX compression score of 8 suggests the price is still in a tight squeeze. The company appears to be in the early stage of a recovery cycle – capitulation may already be complete, but a clear catalyst is still needed to trigger the next move.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: The stock has crashed deeply (over 70 % from peak) and sits on a solid support level with little current interest. If the funding round closes and the Kyrgyz asset shows progress, the price could double or even triple as the market reverts to fundamentals. The signal hierarchy shows that a deep crash (green) offers a 39 % average return historically, and the lack of institutional accumulation signals a ripe opportunity for contrarian entry.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company faces a realistic risk of falling short on its capital needs, regulatory delays, and the possibility that the Kyrgyz deposit is less recoverable than expected. If the funding fails or production milestones are missed, the stock could slide further, potentially below the current support level.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside is roughly 200–300 % if the price reaches the 8.50p level, while downside is limited to the 5–10 % below the entry support. The asymmetry is favorable: a small loss could be offset by a large gain.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter on a bounce to the 7.99p support, size the position to 5–10 % of your capital, and set a stop 5 % below the support. Watch for volume spikes and any announcement of a new partnership or funding close.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash and monitor the next funding announcement or any regulatory approval. A sudden volume spike above 200 % of average daily volume paired with a price break above 8.30p would be the trigger to buy.
playbook.if_passing: None identified – there are no imminent delisting or bankruptcy risks.

ai_insights.bottom_line: With a deep crash and no current catalysts, AMG.L presents a high‑asymmetry contrarian play that will pay off only if the funding round closes and the Kyrgyz asset proves viable.
